title: CLI decision.v1.schema.json diverges from standard: stricter additionalProperties and timestamp formats
outcome: The CLI retains its stricter version of decision.v1.schema.json (additionalProperties: false on all sub-objects, format: date-time on timestamps, links.pr as array-only) rather than reverting to the standard's looser version. A proposal to upstream these changes to provenancecode-standard is deferred to a separate contribution.
rationale: The CLI's tighter schema was introduced during v2.2.0 hardening to enforce data quality. Reverting to the standard's additionalProperties: true would allow garbage fields in records created by prvc. The divergence is intentional and documented here. The stricter version is the desired direction for the standard; it should be proposed upstream rather than silently reverted.

risk.description: Records created by other ecosystem tools (Verity, AgentOS) with extra top-level fields will fail CLI validation. This is an acceptable trade-off as prvc is the reference implementation and these tools are not yet in production use against this CLI version.

problem: The provenancecode-standard repo uses additionalProperties: true and does not enforce format: date-time on timestamps. The CLI tightened both during v2.2.0 security hardening without a corresponding standard change.
